text.title: previous_response_id Reasoning Reuse Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for reusing reasoning items via OpenAI Responses API previous_response_id.

rule.statement: Multi-turn Responses API calls MUST pass previous_response_id from the prior turn's response.id.
rule.rationale: Without it, server-side reasoning items are discarded and the model re-derives them at full cost.

rule.statement: previous_response_id MUST be stored per-conversation in a session store keyed by user_id+conversation_id. Sharing ids across conversations is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Leaking ids across users surfaces reasoning from another conversation.

rule.statement: On 404 (id expired or invalid), the spec MUST fall back to a fresh Responses call without previous_response_id, NOT reconstruct a Chat Completions message array.
rule.rationale: Responses API retention is bounded; the fallback path keeps the agent live.

rule.statement: The spec MUST document retention_ttl_hours from OpenAI policy and treat ids older than that as invalid before sending.
rule.rationale: Local pre-flight check saves a round-trip 404.

rule.statement: Every turn MUST log (user_id, conversation_id, previous_response_id, new_response_id, turn_n) for audit and replay.
rule.rationale: Without per-turn id logs, replay debugging is impossible.

rule.statement: When api_flavor == 'responses' AND multi_turn == true, the spec MUST use previous_response_id.
rule.rationale: This is the recommended continuity primitive.

rule.statement: When api_flavor == 'chat' OR single_turn == true, the spec MUST NOT use previous_response_id (incompatible).
